summary.intent: Display human vs AI authorship in search results using avatars and names, matching the visual style of the single-session conversation view.
summary.outcome: Search results now show GitHub avatars/initials and git usernames for human results, and the Claude logo for assistant results, with content-type badges and distinct background colors per author type.

repo: Search results are built in src/search.ts and require joining back to LogEvent and Session tables to get logEventType and user info.
The SearchResult type is defined in app/src/renderer/src/api.ts and must be updated to include new backend fields.
The single-session UI already has avatar/name display patterns that Search.tsx can mirror for consistency.
tool_result content type is treated as Claude-side (assistant-initiated) even though it appears in the user message role.
The backend server runs as handler.js serve and must be restarted to pick up query changes.

code.finding: SQL query was updated to join LogEvent and Session to expose logEventType and gitUserName on search results.
SearchResult type extended with logEventType and gitUserName fields.
Result cards differentiate by logEventType: assistant gets Claude logo + orange label; user text gets GitHub avatar + indigo label; tool_result gets teal dot-badge.

workflow: Backend changes require explicit server restart (pkill + relaunch) — no hot reload.
Used a quick curl + python3 inline script to validate new API fields before touching the frontend.
TypeScript noEmit checks run separately for backend and frontend to isolate type errors.

friction: Manual server restart required after every backend query change, adding friction to the iteration loop.
open_items: No fallback handling documented if gitUserName is null/empty beyond 'initials fallback' — edge cases may need polish.
tool_result authorship attribution (treated as Claude-side) is a design assumption that may need revisiting if tool results from human-run tools are added.
